matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
seeded_markers: rs1042522 (TP53 Arg72Pro) (TP53 p.Arg72Pro apoptosis/cancer association marker)
TP53 loss-of-function model (TP53 high-impact tumor-suppressor loss model)
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a TP53 stress-response thesis: the individual may carry a marker relevant to DNA-damage checkpoint tone, apoptosis, cell-cycle arrest, senescence, cancer cohort biology, or high-impact tumor-suppressor review depending on the exact allele class. Do not merge benign/common TP53 markers with pathogenic germline or somatic hotspot interpretation without external classification.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: GT-confirmed rs1042522 (TP53 Arg72Pro) dosage suggests this sample carries the TP53 prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests a TP53 stress-response thesis: the individual may carry a marker relevant to DNA-damage checkpoint tone, apoptosis, cell-cycle arrest, senescence, cancer cohort biology, or high-impact tumor-suppressor review depending on the exact allele class. Do not merge benign/common TP53 markers with pathogenic germline or somatic hotspot interpretation without external classification. This specific marker is curated as TP53 p.Arg72Pro apoptosis/cancer association marker. rs1042522 (TP53 Arg72Pro) is curated as a TP53 marker for Cancer susceptibility and tumor-suppressor biology, DNA damage response and apoptosis. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the DNA damage checkpoint, p53 transcriptional control, apoptosis, cell-cycle arrest, and senescence pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched rs1042522 (TP53 Arg72Pro) at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the TP53 prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Can modify or tag DNA damage checkpoint, p53 transcriptional control, apoptosis, cell-cycle arrest, and senescence.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a TP53 stress-response thesis: the individual may carry a marker relevant to DNA-damage checkpoint tone, apoptosis, cell-cycle arrest, senescence, cancer cohort biology, or high-impact tumor-suppressor review depending on the exact allele class. Do not merge benign/common TP53 markers with pathogenic germline or somatic hotspot interpretation without external classification. An observed TP53 variant places this sample in the curated TP53 research context. The local TP53 bundle is tumor-suppressor and DNA-damage-response oriented. Germline pathogenic variants require clinical review for Li-Fraumeni syndrome context, while common and somatic markers should stay separated from germline risk interpretation. TP53 interpretation depends heavily on variant class, zygosity, tumor versus germline origin, and whether the event affects the DNA-binding domain or regulatory regions.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: Li-Fraumeni syndrome and somatic oncology context

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a TP53 variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests a TP53 stress-response thesis: the individual may carry a marker relevant to DNA-damage checkpoint tone, apoptosis, cell-cycle arrest, senescence, cancer cohort biology, or high-impact tumor-suppressor review depending on the exact allele class. Do not merge benign/common TP53 markers with pathogenic germline or somatic hotspot interpretation without external classification. TP53 methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a DNA damage checkpoint, p53 transcriptional control, apoptosis, cell-cycle arrest, and senescence gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data. The local TP53 bundle is tumor-suppressor and DNA-damage-response oriented. Germline pathogenic variants require clinical review for Li-Fraumeni syndrome context, while common and somatic markers should stay separated from germline risk interpretation. TP53 interpretation depends heavily on variant class, zygosity, tumor versus germline origin, and whether the event affects the DNA-binding domain or regulatory regions.
